title: Battery Fault or Overheating
shortDescription: The solid red light indicates a battery fault, overheating, or a critical power issue.
fullDescription: A solid red LED on Dyson cordless vacuums (V8 through V15) indicates a serious battery issue. The battery may be overheating, has a failed cell, or the battery management system has detected an unsafe condition. The vacuum will not operate until the condition is resolved.

causes.cause: Battery has overheated from extended use
Battery cells degraded beyond safe operation

symptoms: Solid red LED on the vacuum body or battery
Vacuum does not turn on when trigger is pulled
Vacuum shut off during use and now shows red
Battery feels warm or hot to the touch
Vacuum was working fine but suddenly stopped

fixSteps.title: Let the vacuum cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the vacuum from the charger. Place it in a cool, well-ventilated area and wait 30-60 minutes for the battery to cool completely.

fixSteps.title: Check the battery connection
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery pack by pressing the release button. Inspect the contacts for debris or corrosion. Reinsert the battery firmly until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Try charging again
fixSteps.instruction: Place the vacuum on the wall-mount charger or plug in the charging cable directly. A solid blue light should appear indicating charging. If the red light returns, the battery may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: If the red light persists after cooling and reseating, the battery pack has failed. Order a genuine Dyson replacement battery for your model and install it by pressing the release button to remove the old one.

whenToCallPro: If a new battery still shows the red light, the main board may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless vacuum solid red light battery fix

faq.q: How long does a Dyson battery last?
faq.a: Typically 2-5 years depending on usage frequency and charging habits.

faq.q: Can I use a third-party battery?
faq.a: Third-party batteries are available but may not have Dyson's BMS safety features. Use at your own risk.

faq.q: Is a hot battery dangerous?
faq.a: A warm battery during or after use is normal. If it is too hot to hold, let it cool completely before charging.

title: Battery Not Charging or Temporary Fault
shortDescription: A flashing red LED indicates the battery is not charging or has a temporary fault.
fullDescription: A flashing or pulsing red LED on Dyson cordless vacuums means the battery is unable to charge. This may be due to an extreme temperature, a docking issue, or a temporary battery fault that can often be resolved by reseating the battery.

causes.cause: Battery temperature too high or too low for charging
Poor connection between battery and charger

symptoms: Red LED flashes on and off
Vacuum does not charge when docked
Flashing starts during charging attempt
Occurs in very hot or cold rooms

fixSteps.title: Move to temperate area
fixSteps.instruction: Place the vacuum and charger in a room with temperature between 50-95F (10-35C).

fixSteps.title: Reseat the battery
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinsert the battery pack firmly.

fixSteps.title: Check the charger
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the charger cable is undamaged and firmly plugged into both the wall and the vacuum. Try a different outlet.

fixSteps.title: Try direct charging
fixSteps.instruction: If using the wall dock, try charging directly with the cable to the vacuum body to rule out a dock issue.

whenToCallPro: If the flashing persists with a known-good charger and a new battery.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless vacuum flashing red light charging fix

faq.q: Why does my Dyson flash red when I plug it in?
faq.a: Usually a temperature issue. Let the vacuum acclimate to room temperature before charging.

faq.q: How long should a Dyson take to charge?
faq.a: 3.5-5 hours depending on the model and battery size.

faq.q: Can a faulty charger cause the flashing red?
faq.a: Yes. Try a different Dyson charger if available to rule this out.

title: Blockage Detected or Airflow Restriction
shortDescription: The vacuum has detected a blockage in the airways or the filter is heavily clogged.
fullDescription: When the Dyson cordless vacuum flashes blue 10 times and cuts out, it indicates a blockage somewhere in the airflow path, from the cleaner head through the wand, body, and filter. The motor protection system shuts down to prevent overheating.

causes.cause: Blockage in the cleaner head or wand
Blockage at the cyclone inlet or bin entry

symptoms: Blue LED flashes 10 times then vacuum cuts out
Suction feels weak before shutting off
High-pitched sound during operation
Vacuum runs for a few seconds then stops

fixSteps.title: Check for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Detach the cleaner head and wand. Look through each piece for visible blockages. Use a long, thin object to push through any debris.
tools: long thin stick or broom handle

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter (typically at the rear of the body). Wash it under cold running water until the water runs clear. Do NOT use detergent. Let it air dry for at least 24 hours.

fixSteps.title: Empty and clean the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the clear bin. Wipe the bin and cyclone mesh with a dry cloth. Check the bin entry point for compacted debris.

fixSteps.title: Test without attachments
fixSteps.instruction: Run the vacuum with no attachments connected (just the body). If it runs fine, the blockage is in the head or wand.

whenToCallPro: If no blockage is found and the filter is clean and dry.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson V10 V11 V15 flashing blue 10 times blockage fix

faq.q: How often should I wash the Dyson filter?
faq.a: Dyson recommends washing the filter at least once a month.

faq.q: Can I use a hair dryer to dry the filter faster?
faq.a: No. Heat can damage the filter material. Air dry only for at least 24 hours.

faq.q: What if I cannot find the blockage?
faq.a: Try running each component separately to isolate where the restriction is.

title: Battery Low or Depleted
shortDescription: The battery charge is too low to continue operation.
fullDescription: A pulsing or breathing blue light means the battery has been depleted and needs recharging. The vacuum will stop operating until recharged.

causes.cause: Battery capacity reduced due to age
Using MAX power mode draining battery quickly

symptoms: Blue light pulses slowly then vacuum stops
Runtime shorter than expected
Vacuum cuts out mid-use
Only runs for a few minutes on full charge

fixSteps.title: Charge the vacuum
fixSteps.instruction: Place the vacuum on the charger and allow a full charge cycle (3.5-5 hours depending on model).

fixSteps.title: Use ECO or Auto mode
fixSteps.instruction: If battery life is short, use ECO or Auto mode instead of MAX. MAX mode uses 3-5 times more power.

fixSteps.title: Check filter condition
fixSteps.instruction: A clogged filter makes the motor work harder, draining the battery faster. Clean the filter monthly.

fixSteps.title: Replace aging battery
fixSteps.instruction: If runtime has dropped significantly from when new, the battery needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: Not usually needed. Battery replacement is straightforward.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless vacuum pulsing blue light low battery fix

faq.q: How long should a Dyson V15 last on one charge?
faq.a: Up to 60 minutes in ECO mode, about 10-12 minutes in MAX mode with a motorized head.

faq.q: Should I keep my Dyson on the charger?
faq.a: Yes, Dyson vacuums are designed to be stored on the charger. The BMS prevents overcharging.

faq.q: Why does my Dyson only last 5 minutes?
faq.a: Check if you are in MAX mode, which drains quickly. If in ECO mode with short runtime, the battery likely needs replacement.

title: Vacuum Does Not Power On
shortDescription: No LED lights appear and the vacuum does not respond to the trigger.
fullDescription: When a Dyson cordless vacuum shows no lights at all and does not respond to the trigger, the issue is typically a completely dead battery, a faulty trigger switch, or a disconnected battery.
symptoms: No lights at all on the vacuum
Trigger produces no response
Vacuum was stored uncharged for a long time
No charging light when plugged in

fixSteps.title: Charge for at least 3 hours
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the charger directly and leave for at least 3 hours. A deeply discharged battery may take time before any LED appears.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the battery
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery, wait 30 seconds, and firmly reinsert it.

fixSteps.title: Try a different charger
fixSteps.instruction: If available, test with a known-good Dyson charger to rule out a charger fault.

fixSteps.title: Check trigger mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Pull and release the trigger. If it feels loose, does not click, or stays depressed, the trigger switch may have failed.

whenToCallPro: If the vacuum does not respond after charging with a known-good charger and battery.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless vacuum not turning on no power fix

faq.q: Can a completely dead battery be revived?
faq.a: Sometimes. Leave it on the charger for 3+ hours. If no LED appears, the battery is likely beyond recovery.

faq.q: How long can a Dyson sit uncharged?
faq.a: Lithium batteries can permanently degrade if left fully discharged for months. Store with some charge.

faq.q: Where can I get a Dyson repaired?
faq.a: Contact Dyson support or visit a Dyson Service Center. They offer out-of-warranty repair services.

title: Filter Maintenance Required or Not Installed
shortDescription: The filter is not properly installed or requires cleaning.
fullDescription: A flashing amber or yellow indicator (found on V11 and newer with LCD screens) signals that the filter needs attention. It may not be installed correctly, needs cleaning, or is still damp after washing.

causes.cause: Filter not properly seated after cleaning

symptoms: Amber or yellow filter icon on LCD display
Vacuum power cuts out intermittently
Reduced suction power
Vacuum refuses to start

fixSteps.title: Check filter installation
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter from the rear of the vacuum body. Twist it back in firmly until it clicks into the locked position.

fixSteps.title: Ensure filter is dry
fixSteps.instruction: If you recently washed the filter, it must be completely dry (24+ hours of air drying). A damp filter triggers this warning and can damage the motor.

fixSteps.title: Wash the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter, rinse under cold water, squeeze gently to remove water, and air dry for at least 24 hours.

fixSteps.title: Replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: If the filter is damaged, torn, or no longer cleans effectively, replace it with a genuine Dyson filter.

whenToCallPro: Not needed for filter issues. Always a DIY fix.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson V11 V15 amber yellow filter light fix

faq.q: How do I know if the filter is dry enough?
faq.a: The filter should feel completely dry to the touch with no damp spots. When in doubt, wait another 12 hours.

faq.q: Can I use my Dyson without the filter?
faq.a: No. The filter is essential for trapping fine particles and protecting the motor.

faq.q: How often should I replace the filter?
faq.a: With monthly washing, a Dyson filter typically lasts 1-2 years before needing replacement.

title: No Suction / Motor Protection Activation
shortDescription: The motor has detected insufficient suction and activated protection mode.
fullDescription: Three blue flashes indicate the motor protection has engaged due to an airflow problem. This is a less severe blockage than 10 flashes, often caused by a partially blocked intake or a cleaner head issue.

causes.cause: Partial blockage in the cleaner head

symptoms: Blue LED flashes 3 times then stops
Motor seems to pulse
Suction is noticeably reduced
Brush bar has visible hair wrapped around it

fixSteps.title: Clean the brush bar
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cleaner head and use scissors to cut away hair and fibers tangled around the brush bar. On V15, use the coin slot to remove the brush bar.

fixSteps.title: Empty the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the clear bin and check for debris at the intake point.

fixSteps.title: Check the wand for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Detach the wand and look through it. Clear any visible blockage.

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and wash the filter if it has not been cleaned recently.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed. This is always a blockage or maintenance issue.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless vacuum 3 blue flashes fix brush bar cleaning

faq.q: Why does my Dyson keep cutting out after a few seconds?
faq.a: A clogged filter or partial blockage is the most common cause. Clean the filter and check all airways.

faq.q: How often should I clean the brush bar?
faq.a: Every 1-2 weeks depending on usage, especially if you have long hair or pets.

faq.q: Can I use the V15 Detect brush bar tool?
faq.a: Yes, the V15 brush bar can be removed using a coin in the end cap slot for easy cleaning.

title: Cleaner Head Brush Bar Has Stopped Spinning
shortDescription: The brush bar in the cleaner head is not rotating.
fullDescription: The cleaner head brush bar has stopped spinning, either due to a tangled brush, a motorhead electrical fault, or a thermal cutout activation. On V11 and newer, a brush bar icon appears on the LCD.

causes.cause: Brush bar jammed with hair and debris
Brush bar thermal cutout activated from overheating

symptoms: Brush bar icon on LCD (V11/V15)
Suction works but brush does not spin
Hair visibly wound around the brush bar
Reduced cleaning performance on carpet

fixSteps.title: Remove the cleaner head
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the cleaner head from the wand.

fixSteps.title: Remove and clean the brush bar
fixSteps.instruction: Use a coin or flat screwdriver to turn the brush bar end cap and remove the brush bar. Cut away all tangled hair and fibers.

fixSteps.title: Let the motorhead cool
fixSteps.instruction: If the brush bar was jammed during use, the thermal cutout may have tripped. Wait 30 minutes for it to cool and reset.

fixSteps.title: Check the electrical connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the cleaner head clicks firmly onto the wand. Check the electrical contacts for debris.

whenToCallPro: If the cleaner head motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless brush bar not spinning fix cleaner head

faq.q: What is the thermal cutout?
faq.a: A safety feature that shuts off the brush bar motor if it overheats. It resets automatically after cooling.

faq.q: Can I buy just the brush bar?
faq.a: Yes, replacement brush bars are available separately from the full cleaner head.

faq.q: Why does my brush bar keep jamming?
faq.a: Long hair is the primary cause. Clean the brush bar every 1-2 weeks to prevent buildup.

title: LCD Screen Displays Blockage Warning
shortDescription: The LCD screen shows a blockage icon with animated arrows indicating restricted airflow.
fullDescription: On V11, V12, and V15 models with LCD screens, a dedicated blockage icon appears showing animated arrows and the affected area. The screen provides visual guidance on where the blockage is located.

causes.cause: Blockage in the location indicated on screen
Multiple partial blockages reducing total airflow
Cyclone mesh blocked with fine dust

symptoms: Blockage icon appears on LCD with location indicator
Arrows animate showing restricted area
Suction power drops
Vacuum may cut out

fixSteps.title: Identify the blockage location
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the LCD screen. The animated icon shows whether the blockage is in the wand, bin inlet, or filter area.

fixSteps.title: Clear the indicated area
fixSteps.instruction: Detach the component shown on screen and clear the blockage. Use a long stick for wand blockages.

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter
fixSteps.instruction: If the filter area is indicated, remove and wash the filter.

fixSteps.title: Check all airways
fixSteps.instruction: Even if one area is shown, check the entire airway from head to filter for additional partial blockages.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed. Follow the LCD guidance.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson V11 V15 LCD blockage icon screen fix

faq.q: What do the LCD icons mean?
faq.a: The V11 and newer models have animated icons showing battery life, power mode, blockage locations, and maintenance reminders.

faq.q: Is the LCD blockage detection accurate?
faq.a: It indicates the general area of restriction. You may still need to check the entire airway.

faq.q: Can the LCD screen be replaced if damaged?
faq.a: Yes, but it requires partial disassembly. Contact Dyson support for repair options.

title: Electrical Fault or Motor Error
shortDescription: A rapid blue flashing pattern indicates an electrical or motor fault.
fullDescription: Rapid or continuous blue flashing that does not match the normal battery or blockage patterns suggests an electrical fault in the motor, wiring, or control board. The motor may have a winding fault or the hall sensor has failed.
symptoms: Blue LED flashes rapidly and continuously
Motor makes unusual noise or does not spin
Vacuum starts briefly then stops with rapid flashing
Burning or electrical smell

fixSteps.title: Stop using immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If you notice a burning smell, stop using the vacuum immediately and do not charge it.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the battery
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinsert the battery. Sometimes a connection issue causes rapid flashing.

fixSteps.title: Try a different battery
fixSteps.instruction: If available, try a known-good battery to isolate whether the issue is the battery or the motor/board.

fixSteps.title: Contact Dyson support
fixSteps.instruction: Rapid flashing with motor issues typically requires professional repair. Contact Dyson for warranty service or repair options.

whenToCallPro: Immediately if the issue is not resolved by reseating the battery.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless vacuum rapid blue flashing motor fault fix

faq.q: Is a burning smell from my Dyson dangerous?
faq.a: Stop using it immediately. A burning smell indicates an electrical fault. Do not charge until inspected.

faq.q: Can the motor be replaced?
faq.a: On most Dyson cordless models, the motor is integrated into the main body and is not easily user-serviceable.

faq.q: Is this covered under warranty?
faq.a: Dyson offers a 2-year warranty (5 years on some models). Motor faults within the warranty period are typically covered.

title: Dust Detection Sensor Malfunction (V15 Detect)
shortDescription: The piezoelectric dust detection sensor is not reporting particle counts.
fullDescription: The V15 Detect features a piezoelectric sensor that counts and sizes dust particles in the airflow. When this sensor malfunctions, the particle count display on the LCD will not update, and the Auto mode will not adjust suction based on dust levels.

causes.cause: Dust buildup on the piezo sensor element

symptoms: Particle count display shows zero or dashes
Auto mode does not adjust suction when vacuuming dirty areas
LCD bar graph remains static
Normal suction but no particle detection

fixSteps.title: Clean around the sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: The piezo sensor is inside the bin inlet area. Use compressed air to blow out fine dust from around the bin entry point.

fixSteps.title: Empty and clean the dustbin thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the bin and wipe the inside surfaces, paying attention to the area near the cyclone inlet where the sensor sits.

fixSteps.title: Restart the vacuum
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the battery for 30 seconds and reinsert. This resets the sensor system.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware via MyDyson app
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the vacuum to the MyDyson app and check for firmware updates that may address sensor calibration issues.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor does not respond after cleaning and firmware update.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson V15 Detect dust sensor not working fix particle count

faq.q: What does the Detect sensor show?
faq.a: It shows real-time particle counts categorized by size, displayed as a bar graph on the LCD screen.

faq.q: Does the V12 have the same sensor?
faq.a: The V12 Detect Slim also has a dust detection sensor, though with a different display format.

faq.q: Can I clean in Auto mode without the sensor?
faq.a: Auto mode will still work but without dynamic suction adjustment. It will default to a standard suction level.

title: Normal Charging Indicator - Troubleshooting Slow Charge
shortDescription: The solid blue light during charging is normal, but a charge that takes too long indicates an issue.
fullDescription: A solid blue LED while charging is the normal indicator. However, if the vacuum takes significantly longer than the expected 3.5-5 hours for a full charge, there may be an issue with the charger, battery, or ambient temperature.

causes.cause: Battery cells degraded, taking longer to charge
Ambient temperature too high or low
Poor electrical connection at the charging port

symptoms: Solid blue light but charge takes more than 6 hours
Battery never reaches full charge
Charging indicator stays on indefinitely
Vacuum works after charging but with reduced runtime

fixSteps.title: Check the charging environment
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the charging area is between 50-95F (10-35C). Extreme temperatures slow charging significantly.

fixSteps.title: Clean the charging port
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to clean the charging port on the vacuum and ensure the charger plug makes a solid connection.

fixSteps.title: Try a different outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Use a different wall outlet. Low-voltage outlets or overloaded circuits can slow charging.

fixSteps.title: Test with a new charger
fixSteps.instruction: If available, try a different Dyson charger to rule out a degraded charger.

whenToCallPro: If charging is abnormally slow with a new charger and battery.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson cordless vacuum slow charging fix blue light

faq.q: How long should a full Dyson charge take?
faq.a: V8: 5 hours, V10: 3.5 hours, V11: 4.5 hours, V15: 4.5 hours.

faq.q: Does the blue light turn off when fully charged?
faq.a: Yes, the blue LED turns off when the battery is fully charged.

faq.q: Can I leave my Dyson on the charger all the time?
faq.a: Yes. The battery management system prevents overcharging.
